194.right:
January 1929

This Jirga, which included Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Abdul Akbar Khan, Rustam Khan, and other Pashtuns, began in the month of November. In this meeting, it was decided to form the Pashtun "Zalmo Jirga" (Youth Jirga), similar to the "All India Youth League" group in India. The primary purpose of forming this Jirga was to show Pashtuns the path of national unity, independence, and the struggle for their rights. In this meeting, Pashtun leaders emphasized in their speeches that Pashtuns must adopt the path of unity and strive for a common goal. The leaders of this Jirga told the participants that without unity, Pashtuns cannot achieve their rights and must support each other in all circumstances for this goal. Likewise, decisions were made in this meeting to strengthen the cultural and social affairs of the Pashtuns. It is worth noting that the message of unity was spread in the meetings of this Jirga, and it guided Pashtuns to rise as a united force. In the course of this Jirga's work, the need to increase efforts for unity and consensus among the youth in various regions of Pashtunkhwa was mentioned. ---
Pashtun 29

This - that a Pashtun does not recognize it, its profit and loss seem good to him. And if someone understands this fully, then they should look at it with a little more care and thought. For this, it is necessary that there should be much more understanding among the people of the Frontier Province regarding this cliché. Compared to the group in India called the (All India Youth League), there are very distinct differences in the culture and politics of our Pashtun Zalmo Jirga. We know that this is a very necessary task for the Pashtuns, that the Jirga which is for a Pashtun province should have discussions and consultations once again with the people of Pashtunkhwa regarding the creation of such a framework for Pashtun politics and its demands. At this time, when Pashtuns take this responsibility upon themselves for their unity, it is clearly a sign of great pride. For the formation of this group, there is a need for a new and strong unity for the Pashtuns at this time. There is a broad participation of the people in the formation of this Jirga, and the contribution of every single person of the nation in this is acceptable as a national duty. In the formation of this Jirga, it acts as a foundation upon which a step is being taken towards a new phase for the future of the Pashtuns. In September 1929, it was decided to lay the foundation of such a Jirga for the people of the Frontier Province, and in October, it was decided to hold a meeting to form a central Jirga, and a decision on such a form for this Jirga in the province is necessary.

197.right:
On January 25th, there was a meeting in the main mosque in Tank, where people were present, and the respected Khans of the area were also included. In the meeting, we conveyed the message of brotherhood to everyone; with sincerity, we found friendship and affection. On January 12th, people were gathered in a meeting in Leech, and sincere workers from Kohat also took the trouble to come; upon their request for prayer, I reached Peshawar.

On December 12th, I set out again, and to the areas of Badarga, Eiro, the Rani Zani of the area, Kot, Miana, Kot, and Spen Khwar, I went to the valleys and Alindai Dara. There is no doubt that the people are ready, and due to some workers, there is preparation; in Badarga, one gives [support], when these special zones are present. In Miana, due to the workers, a special awakening is emerging; it is possible that in Miana, where Abdul Latif Sahib Afandi and his like-minded people are, an independent Islamic school is also running. I took an examination of the students of these members and these Maliks; the boys were very capable.
197.left:
January 1923

33

And set out on the path; the tour on white donkeys ended, but the tour was very difficult, as the journey was always on mountain passes, and then through very harsh Pashtun deserts; besides this, we were not spared from the rain and the donkey's stubbornness.

Although the middle region is mountainous, the mountains are still inhabited, and the land provides all the necessary supplies for a suitable livelihood; there is also plenty of game here—partridges, chukars, and other things are abundant, and at one time the people of this region lived entirely off this. For a long time, the government's attention has been turned toward these people in a crooked way—there is no doubt about it. If they want to hunt, they are fined five rupees per head for many people, and if they want to hunt small game, there is no prohibition; but if they want to hunt in that place, there is no permission for anything unfair, so that they are deprived of the rights of the land, while outsiders hunt freely. May God show favor to these poor Pashtuns, and may God guide their tribal elders not to sell the freedom, peace, prosperity, and honor of their land to others, not to be swayed by the influence of others, and to form their own jirga for the sake of their own reconciliation.

199.right:
January 1920

I say amen to this statement of my Pashtun brothers of the American nation, who give much money in the path of noble service and love!

The truth is that today, work cannot be done without money. You see, today, any nation that does not have a strong treasury (Bait-ul-Mal) does not have new propaganda, hospitals, and schools for its nation, and cannot serve the country and religion of its people; and if a nation does not have a treasury, then you see that it is equal to nothing else, which is the state of the nation.

If a person asks why Pashtuns do not do as other nations do, this statement is wrong; Pashtuns give much more charity than their means. But their method of charity is such that it is not of immediate benefit to the nation, it is not eternal; undoubtedly, their method of charity is in the form of the past era, but today its benefit is a loss to the nation.

The era has changed; it was necessary for Muslims to have changed too. But they did not change with the era, because instead of benefit, it is a loss for them. In the past, people would serve the nation's religion for free, and you would offer money for the people, you would give it to them; in this way, the service of the nation continued, and the servants also made a living.

But you see today that everything is for money. The era has changed; propaganda, publication, school, imamate—in fact, there is no matter that can be done without money, and such a person will not die who girds his loins to serve the nation for the sake of God. If a person girds his loins, if he is wealthy, like our Pashtuns, he will make a living, but if he is poor, then what?

For this reason, today it is necessary for Muslims to move with the pace of the era, for Pashtuns to form a national treasury with unity like other nations for money, goods, and cash ideas, and to help the nation, and to remove the nation's neediness, and to support the servants of Islam. Because I hope that our Pashtun brothers will imitate the Pashtun brothers of America and will walk in their footsteps.

The day that I was informed by our American brothers about the charity group's page regarding the education of the association, because today the most necessary thing for us is to acquire knowledge, the receipt is below:

203.right:
21 July 1921

Frontier News

Official inauguration of the sugar factory - On 16 July, Honorable Doctor Khan, Prime Minister of the Frontier, inaugurated the sugar factory in Takht-i-Bahi, Mardan district.
On this occasion, government officers and local Khans were present.
After the inauguration, the Prime Minister delivered a brief speech and stated: This factory
was started with four lakh rupees, out of which two lakh rupees
have been contributed by the Frontier government.

Grand Political Conference at Marghuz - On 19 and 20 July
a grand political conference was held in Marghuz, Mardan district, on the weekend
under the presidency of Hussain Ahmad Madani. The purpose of which
was announced after careful consideration. The president of the reception committee, Khan
Abdul Aziz Khan, M.L.A., read a brief
welcome address. Following this, the honorable president of the conference delivered that grand
historical presidential address, every word of which was related to Islam
and national politics. Both sides of the gathering were addressed by the provincial
leader Ghani Afghan Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, who shed light on the Khudai Khidmatgar
movement and other current affairs. In this
gathering, besides thousands of Khudai Khidmatgars, countless people were present.

British Policy
The acceptance of humility and humiliation in the face of the powerful, and arrogance and violence in the face of weak nations, is clearly explained by past events. During the British occupation of the Rhineland and the Treaty of Sèvres, Italy occupied Abyssinia, and regarding Japan, Germany made an understanding with the Soviet Union contrary to the Anti-Comintern Pact. In the joint Italian and German involvement in the Spanish Civil War, the British government's stance was evident. Following the defeat of the republican government, all the despicable actions of the Franco government are akin to poisonous stings. Japan's blatant humiliation of the British in China is a testament to Britain's decline. The British policy of non-interference is apparent to the whole world, yet Britain has accepted every instance of humiliation and has stood by watching every kind of tragedy.

Twelve pieces of advice from Japanese mothers to their daughters at the time of marriage

(1) After marriage, do not consider your mother-in-law as anything other than your own mother. In fact, consider your mother-in-law as your own mother, consider her as your own, and in any situation, do not upset her.
(2) After marriage, when you enter someone's land, consider obedience to them as your duty.
(3) Try not to give your husband a chance to complain.
(4) Do not hold any kind of grudge or enmity with the people of the house. This will ruin your life.
(5) If your husband speaks harshly about something, do not argue back. Instead, try to calm his anger.
(6) Maintain good behavior with your husband's relatives. Do not interfere in every matter or his personal affairs.
(7) Wake up early in the morning and stay awake until late at night. And whatever has happened during the day, do not share it in any gathering.
(8) Manage the household affairs with careful thought. Make it your own habit.
(9) Do not tell the West about the state of Japanese upbringing and the condition of the home.

Advice to the student
(Pashto Master, Mannan School, Bannu)
1- Boy! Pursue your interest in Pashto with the intention of completing your education.
2- Do not let yourself make mistakes, or you will lead a purposeless life tomorrow.
3- Understanding everything is necessary; the decline of life and wealth lies in ignorance. If you do not understand this, you will remain deprived of the world for a long time.
4- Seek knowledge and art; if you act well in your own time, you will be blessed with the fortune of this world and the hereafter.
5- Use knowledge and wisdom in every task, for success lies in this.
6- Read literature in your books, as it is also necessary in education.
7- Have a passion for studying beautiful books so that you may understand knowledge and art.
8- Follow the path of Pashto in your work so that you may maintain your place in the world.
9- Now you know what is needed for Pashto education; serve your country in the light of knowledge.
10- Show your skill in everything and see what is good for your life; take the path of effort for your life.

214.right:
The attitude of Muslims towards the enemies of Islam

The history of Islam's attitude and behavior towards the enemies is preserved, what kind of attitude the Muslims had towards the enemies, and what sufferings the Muslims suffered from them, its history is recorded in a safe place. 

The holy religion of Islam, at the beginning of Islam, what kind of oppression was done by the infidels against the master of Islam, Muhammad, peace be upon him, and his companions, on the one hand, with their own lives, and against the infidels, when they were in Makkah, what kind of oppression was done on their lives in a special way, at the same time, what kind of idolatry the people against them were in, so the Prophet (PBUH) always told them about religion. As the owner of Islam in the way of Da'wah and Truth, when they were in war with the infidels and what was their attitude towards them in accordance with the commands of Allah, there is a clear example of the greatness of the holy religion of Islam and its teachings. When the Muslims in Madinah faced the infidels, they always chose their way with patience and tolerance in the face of oppression. Against the enemies, they always acted according to the teachings of Islam. Throughout the history of Islam, the attitude of the Muslims towards the enemies was such that whenever the atrocities of the enemies increased, they would stand firm against their atrocities with patience and tolerance. The stories of their patience and tolerance are preserved in the pages of history. How they treated their enemies according to the teachings of Islam was a sign of their faith and piety. In many cases, in contrast to the gentleness and humbleness of the Muslims, the cruelties and wrongdoings of the enemies have been mentioned very carefully in various parts of history, how the Muslims stood firm for the supremacy of Islam at the time of all kinds of difficulties.
214.left:
In this period of Islamic history, when they were facing infidelity, they lived with great patience. At that time, as an example of enduring the oppression of enemies, when a storm of cruelty and oppression was raised by the infidels against the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and his companions, the Muslims chose the path of patience and tolerance in response. In the face of them, while explaining the word of Allah Almighty and the truth of Islam, they were busy with their invitation work despite all kinds of difficulties. When the oppression of the infidels against the Muslims in Mecca increased, the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) finally migrated from Mecca to Medina Munawara by the command of Allah Almighty. In Medina, a series of conspiracies and plots by the infidels to destroy them was ongoing, but the followers of Islam had prepared to defend the religion of Islam against all kinds of enemy conspiracies. They fought with a kind of faith and courage to protect the religion of Islam against all kinds of hardships caused by the enemies. Anyway, the stories of the enmity of the infidels at the beginning of Islam are very numerous, but the stories of the patience and tolerance of the Muslims are even more numerous, as to how they sacrificed their lives in the path of their Lord, but did not turn away from the path of Islam. In Medina, a conspiratorial group of enemies of Islam, led by Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul, always tried to create a rift in the ranks of Islam, but the Muslims chose the path of unity and solidarity against these conspiracies of the enemies, and they failed their conspiracies with great patience.

relation with man.

235.right: The people of Peshawar declare their desire for freedom. They believe that the moral standards of their teachers and elders are paramount, and that these principles should guide their actions. They urge the government to recognize the importance of education and to support schools, ensuring that future generations receive a proper education.  They express a hope that the government will prioritize the welfare of the people and strive for a just and prosperous future, recognizing the need to address the grievances of the populace and to foster unity and stability within the nation.  They pray that freedom will be established in India, and that figures like Maulana Abdul Mannan, who champion the cause of liberty, will continue to inspire the people.  This declaration, issued on August 1939, reaffirms the commitment of the people of Peshawar to the pursuit of freedom and justice.

In 1890, the Iranian government's granting of the tobacco monopoly to a British company created a very wide field for Sayyid Sahib. To take advantage of this time, Sayyid Sahib sent that famous letter to the supreme Mujtahid of Samarra, Mirza Hassan Shirazi, informing him of the Iranian government's opposition and the waste and extravagance of the common people's wealth for the benefit of the enemies of Islam. In that letter, he wrote: That until now, the profits made on the lines have confirmed the right to increase in the economy. And by granting the monopoly of tobacco, the whole of Iran has been surrendered to the hands of Europe. This letter of Sayyid Sahib, which was sent to the clerics, had a very great impact.

Peace be upon you, and the mercy and blessings of Allah.

values it.

Definition of Simile: It is when two things are compared in a specific quality
to indicate a relationship, it is called (Simile), and among those two things, the first
is called (Mushabbah) and the second is called (Mushabbah bihi), and that specific meaning
which is common between both is called (Wajh-e-Shabah), like Zaid in bravery
is like a lion - in this example, Zaid and the lion share the quality of bravery
Zaid is called Mushabbah - and the lion is called Mushabbah bihi - and the meaning of
bravery is called Wajh-e-Shabah.
(2) Mushabbah and Mushabbah bihi are known either through the senses
or through the intellect - the first is called sensory simile - and the second is called intellectual simile
The first is divided into five parts - first: sensory, second: manufactured, third: from the senses, fourth: from the intellect - details of this
Sensory simile - first: sensory (which is known through the eyes)

And both of these are known through the eyes.

256.right:
Like a murder

Regarding true civilization and education, it is a great necessity for the well-being of the Pashtun and the Pashtun themselves that steps be taken to establish a system at the national level that creates a broad and standard education system for the people of the country. This is a path in which every citizen of the country can obtain their right. For the importance of this education, it is necessary that efforts be made to create a curriculum at the national level that enhances the mental abilities of the people. Its goal should be the promotion of science and knowledge for the progress of the homeland, not just for a specific class. The current system that exists pays attention to the interests of a specific class, due to which many members of our society are left without education. We must try to ensure that education is in accordance with the needs of the people and that every citizen can benefit from the blessing of education in their own country. No matter how many obstacles there are in this path, we must move forward for a bright future and work for the progress of our country in the light of knowledge. Our goal should not just be to build schools, but to create an environment in which everyone has equal opportunity for learning. With this, every individual in our society will participate in the progress of the country and a strong society will be created.
256.left:
A short note on the great need for the development of true civilization and education:

In reality, national education is a necessary step for the common interests of all and the well-being of society. Unfortunately, the current system in the field of education has a series of shortcomings, due to which the people of the country remain deprived of their true right. In these circumstances, a major change is needed in the field of education that will open the doors of knowledge for every citizen of the country. Current education is for a limited circle, while education as a social right should reach everyone. For the development of education, it is necessary that policies be made at the government level that take into account the needs of the people and that practical steps be taken for the progress of the country. In this way, the people of our society will not only be equipped with the light of knowledge, but will also shine in the world as a strong and developed society. It is the duty of every Pashtun to strive for the popularization of education and to raise their voice for this important task.

Science of Rhetoric (Ilm-e-Badi)
Allama Taftazani, in the book named Mutawwal, which is divided into two parts, or used for verbal and semantic purposes, uses these in speech as a form of embellishment, not every one of which is called Ilm-e-Badi. There are two types of Ilm-e-Badi. One is verbal (1) and the other is semantic arts (Sana'i-e-Ma'nawi). One of the types of semantic science is the art of Tibaq (antithesis). I leave the others to the imagination of the aforementioned mentioned above. The art of Tibaq is also called so in books of rhetoric. And this is an art in which two meanings or contrasts are mentioned in speech. This art has three types: (1) Nominal Tibaq, (2) Verbal Tibaq, (3) Particle Tibaq. 282.right:
Pashtun Elementary

A look at Albania

Albania's (former) King "Ahmed Zogu" is one of those figures of the world who, due to his merit and courage, rose above his ordinary status.

The story of this king's death, Jalal Beg, was among the tribes of Albania. Besides the status of his own business, he was the trusted deputy of Sultan Abdul Hamid. He was born in 1895. Ahmed Zogu was raised in Albania in a way that prepared him for leadership. Until 1915, Albania was part of the Turkish government. But when a freedom movement started against the Sultan's poor governance in Turkey, Ahmed Zogu began to harbor thoughts of his country's freedom. At the age of twenty, he joined the movement. He endured exile, imprisonment, and the hardships of war. Eventually, he succeeded in his goal. In 1924 he became the Prime Minister of Albania, and in 1925 he became the head of Albania. Finally, in 1928, due to his merit and power, he was made King of Albania and took the title "Zogu".

During the time of the freedom movement, he faced many enemies. The people of Albania, like the Wazirs, believe in blood feuds. Controlling these bloodthirsty people was not an easy task. Ahmed Zogu was a brave man. He controlled them with an iron fist. He was always in danger from his enemies. There were reports of him being targeted for assassination over three hundred (300) times. Many attempts were made on his life, but fate protected him, and he remained alive.

He kept his palace protected under the cover of machine guns and often did not participate in public gatherings.

Ahmed Zogu was a very learned man. He could speak German, French, Turkish, and Serbian. He was fond of studying history and spent his lonely nights engaged in work for the betterment of the country.

Zogu was very skilled in maintaining law and order among the people. He brought his enemies under control through his good behavior. Whenever he found out that someone intended to rebel, he would invite them to the palace and bring them under control through his polite behavior.

A priest, Fan Noli, was his very staunch enemy. When Zogu came to power, he exiled him. He lived in America. Finally, he became very ill, and for his treatment, financial ---

by the blessing of the nature of participation, he becomes a victim of trials, and in country after country, his own
government was established, as is proven by the history of India.
It should be remembered that Khushal Khan Khattak wanted the evolution of the Pashtun nation
to reach this level; he wanted to cleanse the Pashtun from the curse of internal wars,
and wanted to lead them on the path of national revival and national greatness,
meaning he was trying to make the Pashtun a very strong nation, and in the path
of freeing the Pashtun from the slavery of the Mughals, but alas! He was
unsuccessful in that blessed effort; if he had been successful in his own words,
then perhaps, the blue sky of India and Asia
in the atmosphere, the falcon of the mountains, the Pashtun hawk, would have been seen
flying over everyone's head, and the high star of the Pashtun's fortune and government
would have been visible to everyone.
Now, this national trait of the Pashtun and Pashto has taken another turn,
Pashtunwali, the stone of Pashto does not rot in the water, for the Pashtun's revenge

287.right:
Khushal Khan's poetry, patriotism, and personality:-
Patriotism is poetry, and poetry is patriotism. In these two, the spirit of Afghan patriotism is woven into poetry in such a way that every flower seems to be a symbol of the country's bravery. Sometimes, along with Khushal Khan's poetry, this patriotism is also discussed on appropriate occasions.
Khushal's war poetry - its usage, rhythm, and rhyme stir the heart of every Pashtun. And it stands as a monument to the past of Pashto. These poets wrote that the Pashtun's war and the Pashtun's war-related hopes can be seen. And the thoughts of these warriors are his. In these great works of the era, the theory is that the merits and beauties of these poets are all present. It is a true mirror of Pashtun patriotic poetry. Because it reflects the simple, sharp, and warlike nature of the Pashtun and the state of the country. In this Diwan, the hardships of the Pashtun's life are depicted in every home. In every village, the Pashtun war, the internal conflicts, the oppression of the Khans, the expansion of oil, the instilling of fear, the Pashtun's battles with the Mughals, the destruction of the Pashtun land, the habits and customs of the people, their way of life, chivalry, hospitality, and the standards of other civilizations and cultures, and the identity of the Afghan, moral standards, and warfare.

Moral essays. Poetic descriptions of music. Poetic study of natural scenery. Ghazals of love and affection. Rubaiyat and qita'at in chronograms, etc., poets and myself in this style. 294.left:
Pashtun Utmanzai
21 June 1939
Volume (5) Number (14)
A message of condolence from Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant patience to his family and all his friends. Amen.

295.right:
Pashtun Utmanzai
21 June 1939
Pashtun's message of condolence on the death of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan
Alas and a hundred times alas that today a great leader like Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan, who understood the nation's pain and sorrow, and a fighter for the path of the homeland's freedom who always raised his voice against the oppression and tyranny of the British, is no longer among us. His passing is a great tragedy for the entire nation. In this grief, not only his family and friends but all those who dreamed of the independence and prosperity of the homeland are in mourning. The history of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan's life and struggle is a shining example for those who are prepared to sacrifice their lives for the path of truth and reality. With his death, a page of Pashtunkhwa's struggles has been turned. May God have mercy on him and grant him a place in Paradise. We express our sympathy to his family and survivors in this grief.
295.left:
A message of sympathy for the Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant his family and all his friends the patience to bear this. Amen.

Flood devastation in Bulgaria
News has arrived that due to heavy rains in the territory of Bulgaria, a very frightening flood has occurred, and it has made many people in Bulgaria homeless, and many people were destroyed in it.

Russia, the West, and the treaty
Like in the past, there is talk of a treaty between Russia and the West, and it is said that Russia's conditions towards the West are leaning towards being very harsh. According to England and Lithuania, a series of negotiations among the people of the West is about to begin. Efforts are being made to improve the political and economic situation in Russia, and they are talking with Western representatives to accept the Russian government's demands in this regard.

Germany under British occupation
Berlin news says that in the parts of Germany that are under the British military, a process of forming a new government has begun, and the German people are prepared for this. Since the German government is busy with peace talks with England and France, at the same time, news is reporting the start of a new government in these areas that were under German control.

Arabs trained their spies
The British government is seeking to secretly start the work of some spies with certain Arab tribes; this is for their own interests. This comes at a time when Arab tribes have shown their reaction to this action by England and have begun their preparations to protect their regions. In this way, a series of tensions have arisen between the Arab tribes and British intelligence.

News of the Frontier

Inauguration ceremony of the sugar factory - On 16 July, Honorable Doctor Khan, Prime Minister of the Frontier, in Takht-i-Bahi, Mardan district, at the sugar factory
At this occasion, government officers and Khans of the area were present.
After the Prime Minister's visit to the factory, in a brief speech, he stated - This factory
was started with four lakh rupees, out of which two lakh rupees
have been taken by the Frontier government.

The grand political conference of Marghuz - On 19 and 20 July
in Marghuz, Mardan district, a grand political conference was held under the presidency of
Husain Ahmad Sahib Madani. Regarding which
the announcement of deliberation and preparation had been made. The president of the reception committee, Khan
Abdul Aziz Khan Sahib, M.L.A., read a brief
address of welcome. After that, the honorable president of the conference delivered that high
historical presidential address, every word of which was related to Islam
and national politics. On both sides of the meeting, the provincial
leader Ghani Afghan Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan Sahib shed light on the Khudai Khidmatgar
movement and other present circumstances. In this
besides thousands of Khudai Khidmatgars, countless people were present.

Mirwais Khan—and now that time is not here—lessons for the Pashtuns

would not be ready to recognize the Pashtuns of Mirwais as their subjects,
and that they were ready for any kind of war and confrontation. Second, that due to
the ambassador's arrogance, the Iranian army's preparations would be exposed—Mirwais's

very close friend of Mirwais, and they had been companions on the journey to Mecca. Being
new and hopeful, the idea was that Muhammad Khan would have a great influence

Pashtun Amanai

Twelve pieces of advice from Japanese mothers to their daughters at the time of marriage

(1) After the marriage, do not consider your mother-in-law as anything other than your own mother. In fact, consider your mother-in-law as your own mother, and consider all your in-laws as your own, and in any situation, do not upset her.
(2) After the marriage, when you are in someone's land, you must consider their obedience as your duty.
(3) Try so that an opportunity for your mother-in-law to complain about you does not arise.
(4) Do not hold any kind of grudge or enmity with the people of the house. This will ruin your life.
(5) If your husband says something harsh about a matter, do not argue back with him. And try to calm his anger.
(6) Maintain good behavior with your husband's relatives. Do not interfere in every task and his personal affairs.
(7) Wake up early in the morning. And stay awake until late at night. And whatever has passed during the day, do not share it in any gathering.
(8) Manage the household affairs with careful thought. Make it your own habit.
(9) Tell the state of the Japanese upbringing and the condition of the daughters to the West.

339.right:
(10) Be consistent with yourself internally and externally and try
that your personality appears in the eyes of everyone.
(11) Wear simple and inexpensive clothes - but take great care of cleanliness
and observe the etiquette of clean clothes.
(12) Do not be arrogant about your father and family's reputation - and with pride
mention them, whether the master is poor or rich, respect them.
(From the literary circle of Khan Sahib Afghan from Senior Peshawar)

Why is Pashtun Imtanzai?

By looking at the history of the Pashtun nation, naturally, in every man's
mind, this question arises: O Pashtun! Why are you so stubborn
that your heart never became pure? Why are you so full of
malice and hatred? You are always ready to pull others down
and are after taking their honor. Not an hour passes that you
do not go after their young men. You blame the past and do not
even spare your own sisters and mothers for your selfishness or pride.

Advice to the student
(Pashto Master, Mannan School, Bannu)
1- Boy! Pursue your passion for Pashto with the intention of completing your education.
2- Do not let yourself make mistakes; otherwise, you will lead a purposeless life tomorrow.
3- Understanding everything is necessary; the decline of life and wealth lies in ignorance. If you do not understand this, you will remain deprived of the world for a long time.
4- Seek knowledge and art; if you act well in your own time, you will be blessed with the fortune of this world and the hereafter.
5- Use knowledge and wisdom in every task, for success lies in this.
6- Read literature in your books, as it is also necessary in education.
7- Have a passion for studying beautiful books so that you may understand knowledge and art.
8- Follow the path of Pashto in your work so that you may maintain your place in the world.
9- Now you know what is needed for Pashto education; serve your country in the light of knowledge.
10- Show your skill in everything and see what is good for your life; choose the path of effort for your life.

343.right:
The attitude of Muslims towards the enemies of Islam

In history, the events of Islamic attitudes and behavior towards enemies are preserved, regarding what kind of attitudes Muslims had towards enemies, and in return for the hardships that Muslims suffered at their hands, the history of that is recorded in a preserved place.

The holy religion of Islam, at the beginning of Islam, the kind of oppression that was committed by the infidels against the owner of Islam, Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions (RA), on one hand with their own lives, and the kind of oppression that was committed against them in their lives in a special way when they were in Makkah, at the same time that people were in idolatry against them, then the Prophet (PBUH) always, as the owner of Islam in the path of invitation to religion and truth, when they were in wars with enemies and the attitude they had towards them according to the commands of Allah Almighty, in that is a clear example of the greatness of the holy religion of Islam and its teachings. When Muslims faced the infidels in Medina, they had always chosen their path with patience and tolerance in the face of oppression and tyranny. In the face of enemies, they always acted according to the teachings of Islam. Throughout the history of Islam, the attitudes of Muslims towards enemies were such that whenever the oppression of the enemies increased, they would stand firm against their oppression with patience and tolerance. The stories of their patience and tolerance are preserved in the pages of history. The fact that they treated their enemies according to the teachings of Islam was a sign of their faith and piety. In many situations, the mention of the enemies' oppression and unjust actions in the face of the Muslims' gentleness and open-mindedness has been recorded with great precision in various parts of history, how Muslims stood firm for the supremacy of Islam by the command of their Lord in times of every difficulty. When a series of conspiracies and plots to eliminate them were going on by the infidels in Medina, the followers of Islam had prepared to defend the religion of Islam against every kind of conspiracy of the enemies. In the face of every kind of hardship from the enemies, they fought with a kind of faith and courage to protect the religion of Islam. Anyway, the stories of the enmity of the infidels at the beginning of Islam are very numerous, but the stories of the patience and tolerance of the Muslims are even more numerous, how they gave their lives in the path of their Lord, but did not turn away from the path of Islam. In Medina, by a conspiratorial group of enemies of Islam led by Abdullah bin Ubayy bin Salul, they always tried to create a rift in the ranks of Islam, but Muslims had chosen the path of unity and solidarity against these conspiracies of the enemies, and had failed their conspiracies with great tolerance.
343.left:
During this period of Islamic history, when they were facing disbelief, they lived with great patience. As an example of enduring the oppression of enemies at that time, when a storm of cruelty and tyranny was raised by the infidels against the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and his companions, the Muslims chose the path of patience and tolerance in response. In the face of them, while explaining the word of Allah Almighty and the truth of Islam, they remained busy with their invitation work despite all kinds of difficulties. When the oppression of Muslims by the infidels in Mecca increased, the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) finally migrated from Mecca to Medina Munawara by the command of Allah Almighty. In Medina, a series of conspiracies and plots by the infidels to destroy them was ongoing, but the followers of Islam had prepared to defend the religion of Islam against all kinds of enemy conspiracies. They fought with a kind of faith and courage to protect the religion of Islam against all kinds of hardships caused by the enemies. Anyway, the stories of the enmity of the infidels in the beginning of Islam are very numerous, but the stories of the patience and tolerance of the Muslims are even more numerous, as to how they sacrificed their lives in the path of their Lord but did not turn away from the path of Islam. In Medina, a conspiratorial group of enemies of Islam, led by Abdullah bin Ubayy bin Salul, always tried to create a rift in the ranks of Islam, but the Muslims chose the path of unity and solidarity against these conspiracies of the enemies, and they failed their conspiracies with great patience.

The third session of the Loya Jirga
The third session of the Loya Jirga began, and its proposals were considered and discussed.
An exchange of views took place among the members of the Jirga regarding the proposals, and the members discussed the topics among themselves. Other members, including Hakim Abdul Karim Sahib, also participated on behalf of the Jirga members and expressed their thoughts on a topic.
In the exchange of views among the Jirga members, other members, including Hakim Abdul Karim Sahib, also expressed their opinions. An exchange of conversation also took place between Ghulam Mohammad Khan and other members of the Jirga. Discussions were held between them on various issues.
According to the Jirga members, some questions were raised regarding certain topics in the Jirga's proposals. The Jirga members tried to clarify the subject further by presenting their views.
Among the Jirga members, including other members, discussions were held regarding new topics in the Jirga's work by presenting the views of some other members.
354.left:
August 19, 39
In the end, the members of the Jirga exchanged views on some important issues and made decisions regarding their proposals. The process of dialogue between the government and the Jirga members regarding their proposals continued. The government assured the Jirga members about its decisions.
In the end, the Jirga members expressed their satisfaction with the government's actions regarding some important issues. At the end of their work, the Jirga members expressed their opinions regarding their proposals, and the Jirga meeting concluded.
The Jirga meeting concluded with some important decisions. The Jirga members requested efforts to implement the government's decisions regarding their proposals.

A letter from a Pashtun brother

Greetings
Our leader Fakhr-e-Afghan Sahib and Awanir Sahib are under the protection of Allah Almighty.
Peace be upon you and all your brothers.
Regarding the news that has reached me about the Pashtun newspaper,
that the Pashtun newspaper has been banned within a week.
It is true that for several years, the Pashtun national newspaper has been struggling.
However, from this point on, a demand has been made from these four publications.
The ears of the officials have been deaf to the patience of the brothers. Among the Pashtuns, this favor
is as much as one hopes for in a week of finding bread. The Pashtun nation is upon it,
listening to the poor. And they do not bring out another dream. Whatever is heard or listened to in the morning,
it is from them. Even if there are such demands in Pashtun, then again
it is not there. Pashtun needs an injection at all times. Pashtun
nation itself is strong, if the Pashtun takes medicine for a hundred years, then he will twist his mustache.
The face of the prominent ones is alive, and they are also praised by the people.
Sher Bahadur Khan is in the embrace of the palace. But my Pashtuns are from the end to the beginning.
Fazal Khan is the center. And the arrival of the guest. Afzal Khan's revenge is in the investigation and trial.
This sentence is closed. They are in one place and Sher Bahadur Khan is the head, who says that the government is coming to us.
With Sher Bahadur Khan, within the scope of the law, the official rules were broken by the writers and
his friends, then again in the Pashtun, he is the occupier of the news as his national newspaper.
Enough, I do not want to take much of your time, I only pray that as long as I am in Pata Mohmand.
What is printed in the name of Pashtun is current and the rest is poetry. The list of free chapters is open
Write that I am a Pashtun, much or little, and whatever I do, God willing, I will bring it to you
Now I am not bound by the week of imprisonment.

Definition of Simile: To indicate that two things share a specific
quality is called (Simile), and among those two things, the first
is called (Mushabbah) and the second is called (Mushabbah bihi), and that specific meaning
which is common between the two is called (Wajh-e-Shabah), like Zaid in courage
is like a lion - in this example, Zaid and the lion share the participation in courage
. Zaid is called Mushabbah - and the lion is called Mushabbah bihi - and the meaning of
courage is called Wajh-e-Shabah.
(2) Mushabbah and Mushabbah bihi are either known through the senses or
through the intellect - the first is called sensory simile - and the second is called intellectual simile
It is of five types - first, sensory; second, manufactured; third, abstract; fourth, sensory; fifth, intellectual - details of this
Sensory Simile - first, sensory (which is known through the eyes)
The beautiful rope of the moon is mentioned in the definition -

The world of truth will be perfumed by its own nature

408.right:
Science of Rhetoric (Ilm-e-Badi)
Allama Taftazani, in the book named Mutawwal, which is divided into two parts, or used for verbal and semantic purposes, uses this type in speech as a form of embellishment, not in every instance, which is called Ilm-e-Badi. Ilm-e-Badi is of two types. One is verbal (1) and the other is semantic arts (Sana'i-e-Ma'nawi). Among the various types of semantic science, one is the art of Tibaq (antithesis). I leave the others to the reader's imagination based on the aforementioned. The art of Tibaq is also mentioned in books of rhetoric. It is an art where two meanings that have opposition or contrast are mentioned in speech. This art has three types: (1) Nominal Tibaq, (2) Verbal Tibaq, (3) Particle Tibaq. The type occurs in the manner of affirmation or in the manner of negation. (1) An example of nominal Tibaq is "Wa tahsabuhum ayqazan wahum ruqud" (And you would think them awake, while they were asleep). The opposition between 'ayqaz' (awake) and 'ruqud' (asleep), or 'hayy' (alive) and 'mayyit' (dead), is nominal Tibaq. Abdul Rahman Sarbini says, and the page of the book, whatever he says is all true, and water in love is nominal Tibaq. Abdul Qadir Wali, with his test and the praise of another, in the translation of the verse, and in the translation, there is nominal Tibaq, which is a Persian verse. To Khudai Fazal Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan Sahib:

[It has been] complicated. [It has been] difficult. May God grant you more [strength] so that the Pashtun nation may become worthy of standing in line with other nations.

Gul Muhammad is after you, his condition is fine, and he sends many greetings to you in your service. ---

The war has finally started

It is clear that the terrifying dust of the Great War, which is the result of the war's events and occurrences, is nothing but destruction in the direction of war. What goes from someone's heart will be forced by events in the war, that furthermore in this place, news from the people with truth about the start of the war... Who started the war? Bun Amantzi

In the arena of the diverse events of the current world, there is no such place that is hidden from everyone's eyes. In the capital of Delhi, at a meeting of Delhi University, a young honorable Professor Dr. Zakir Hussain Sahib delivered such eloquent and rhetorical speeches that stirred everyone's heart and instilled a passion for serving the homeland in everyone's heart. Dr. Sahib said with great love and a feeling of patriotism that: "Our country faces many obstacles on the path of progress, and it is necessary for us to work together to remove these obstacles." Dr. Sahib greatly emphasized the importance of education and training and said that the only way for a nation's progress is education. His words found a place in the hearts of the attendees, and everyone uttered the word "Bravo." Alas, such speakers are very few in our country.
